question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 43.00% on 2026-03-26 for the Metaculus question "Will Anthropic be a designated supply chain risk on May 1, 2026?"?
summary: This meta-prediction asks whether the Metaculus CP for "Will Anthropic be a designated supply chain risk on May 1, 2026?" will be strictly above 43% on March 26. The CP has been declining (50% → 43% from March 7-14), and the threshold is the current level. A court hearing on the preliminary injunction is scheduled for March 24-25, just before resolution. Legal consensus strongly favors Anthropic (experts call the designation "likely unlawful," massive amicus support, no briefs for government). If the court grants an injunction, CP drops well below 43%; if denied, it rises above. The asymmetry of legal evidence and the downward trend favor CP staying at or below 43%, but the proximity to the threshold and possibility of injunction denial create meaningful upside risk. Scenario decomposition yields ~30% probability.

factors.description: CP downward trend: 50% → 43% in one week (March 7 to March 14), suggesting forecasters increasingly believe the designation will be overturned
Court hearing scheduled for March 24-25, before resolution date of March 26. Judges often rule quickly on TRO/PI motions, creating a likely catalyst before the check date

Legal consensus strongly favors Anthropic: multiple legal observers say designation is 'likely unlawful', no amicus briefs support the government, many support Anthropic (Microsoft, researchers, unions, former military)
Manifold market at 81% that Anthropic escapes designation by EOY 2026, suggesting strong market consensus the designation won't survive
Trump administration aggressively defending in court (DOJ filing March 18); courts sometimes defer to executive on national security grounds - could lead to injunction denial pushing CP above 43%
CP is exactly AT the threshold (43.00%) - strict inequality means status quo resolves NO, but any small upward movement crosses the threshold

reasoning:
**Forecast: 30% probability (YES)**

Key reasoning:
- The CP has been declining (50% → 43%) as legal challenges to the supply chain risk designation mount
- A court hearing on Anthropic's injunction request is scheduled for ~March 24-25, just before the March 26 resolution check
- Legal consensus overwhelmingly favors Anthropic (no amicus briefs support the government), making an injunction more likely than not
- If the court grants relief, CP would drop well below 43% (resolving NO); if denied, CP would jump above 43% (resolving YES)
- The asymmetric legal landscape and existing downward trend tilt toward the CP remaining at or below 43%, but there's meaningful (~30%) probability the court denies the injunction or no ruling comes in time with the CP drifting upward

resolution_criteria: This question will resolve based on the community prediction of the Metaculus question found [here](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42491) (i.e. the 'target question'). If the community prediction of the target question on 2026-03-26 12:00:51 is higher than 43.00%, this question will resolve to 'Yes'. If it is lower or equal to 43.00%, this question will resolve to 'No'. If the target question has already resolved before this question opens, then this question will be annulled. If the target question resolves after this question opens, but before 2026-03-26 12:00:51, then this question will resolve to the same value as the target question.
